detailedExemplarData.title: Should Southwest Airlines change its open seating policy to an assigned seat policy?

subject.title: Business Management
subject.description:
**IB Business Management** is a **Group 3** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **business fundamentals, human resource management, finance and accounts, marketing, and operations management**, with a strong focus on **case study analysis, real-world business applications, and strategic decision-making**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2024**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| **Assessment** | **Format** | **SL** | **HL** | **Weighting** |
|----------------|------------|--------|--------|---------------|
| **Paper 1** | Case study-based exam | 1h 30min | 1h 30min | SL: 35%, HL: 25% |
| **Paper 2** | Core themes exam | 1h 30min | 1h 30min | SL: 35%, HL: 30% |
| **Paper 3** | Social enterprise case study | N/A | 1h 15min | HL: 25% |
| **Internal Assessment** | Written commentary (SL) / Research project (HL) | Various | Various | SL: 30%, HL: 20% |

strengths: Key concept of change is defined clearly and contextualized in the introduction
Repeated references to change in analytical sections demonstrate good understanding
Connections to investor pressure and consumer preferences reinforce relevance to Southwest
weaknesses: The concept of change is not explicitly integrated into every framework discussion (e.g., Ansoff and Porter sections)
Occasional generic statements about consumer behaviour are not re-anchored to Southwestâs context
Opportunities to weave âchangeâ more deeply into methodological explanations were missed
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Although the student demonstrates a good understanding of the concept of change with clear analytical thinking, its integration is uneven across all tools and sections. This aligns with the band-3 descriptor: good understanding with clear analytical thinking but lacking full thematic integration throughout.

strengths: Exactly five supporting documents are provided, meeting the IB quantity requirement
Sources include press releases, investor transcripts, news articles and an activist letter, all directly relevant
Documents are cited in the analysis to substantiate key arguments
weaknesses: Heavy reliance on a single author (Hoang) for multiple footnotes limits source diversity
Some documents could have been complemented by primary interviews or company filings
Depth is strong but the range of perspectives is slightly constrained
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The student meets the quantitative and relevance criteria but shows limited diversity in sources. While documents are generally in-depth, the reliance on one recurring author prevents the full breadth of ideas required for the top band, meriting a band-3 score.

strengths: Three well-chosen frameworks (Ansoff, Porterâs Generic Strategies, Hofstede) cover strategic, competitive and cultural dimensions
Each tool is applied to Southwestâs context with clear narrative links
Rationale for selecting these frameworks is stated
weaknesses: Quantitative support (e.g., risk rankings) is not fully substantiated with calculations or sensitivity analysis
There is no discussion of why alternative frameworks (PESTEL, VRIO) were excluded
Occasional leaps in logic reduce theoretical rigor and clarity of relevance
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The selection of tools is appropriate and generally effective, but application lacks consistent clarity and depth. This matches the band-3 descriptor: adequately applied with relevance not always clear.
feedback.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

strengths: Effective use of current market-share figures and financial data to anchor analysis
Sustained integration of supporting documents strengthens arguments
Logical synthesis of findings across frameworks demonstrates mostly effective analysis
weaknesses: Absence of sensitivity testing or counter-arguments limits evaluation depth
Comparative cost data against rivals is suggested but not provided
Methodology section could specify data-validation steps for transparency
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Data selection is sufficient and the analysis is mostly effective, showing strong integration of evidence. Minor gaps prevent the top band, placing it firmly in band 4.

strengths: Conclusions are fully consistent with the evidence presented
The research question is explicitly and directly answered
Key recommendations mirror the main analytical findings
weaknesses: The conclusion largely summarises earlier points rather than offering new insight or reflection
There is little discussion of limitations or areas for further research
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The conclusion meets the highest descriptor for consistency and explicit answer, warranting a band-3 score.

strengths: Logical organization with clearly labeled sections and a comprehensive table of contents
Effective signposting (e.g., âTools usedâ header) enhances coherence throughout
weaknesses: One section (âSourcing & Supporting Documentsâ) cuts off mid-sentence, disrupting flow
Minor navigational clarity issues in the appendix sequencing
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Overall structure is appropriate and coherent throughout, fitting the band-2 descriptor despite small lapses in completeness.

strengths: All required IB elements are included (cover page, TOC, word count, bibliography, appendices)
Figures and tables are legible and captioned, meeting presentation standards
weaknesses: Some awkward phrasing affects readability in places
Graphs lack axis labels or legends for full clarity
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Presentation fulfils all IB requirements and is consistently clear, aligning with the band-2 descriptor. Minimal formatting and clarity issues remain.

strandSelections.reasoning: A logical sequence (introduction, analysis, conclusion, bibliography, appendices) with a detailed table of contents demonstrates appropriate organisation throughout, meriting the top band for structure.

The documents comprise press releases, investor transcripts, reputable news articles and a hedge-fund letter, all directly relevant to the research question and providing multiple viewpoints (internal, external, activist). Their content is cited in the analysis to support arguments, showing depth and range; hence the highest band for relevance and depth.

The IA includes cover page details, table of contents, word count, bibliography and labelled supporting documents, fulfilling all IB required elements; therefore, the highest presentation band applies.
Exactly five supporting documents are listed and clearly labelled in the appendix, meeting the 3-5 requirement and securing the top band for quantity.

Each tool is applied to Southwestâs situation with clear narrative links, but quantification is light and some assertions (e.g., risk level âmoderately lowâ) lack supporting calculations. The relevance is generally clear, yet occasional leaps in logic reduce rigour. This aligns with âadequately applied but relevance not always clearâ for 3 marks.

Data drawn include market-share figures, revenue, loss amounts, survey percentages and cost references. Although not exhaustive, these data points are sufficient and well-integrated into arguments, demonstrating âsufficient selection and useâ (4 marks).

The analysis links each framework to the research question and synthesises findings in the conclusion. While not deeply numerical, the reasoning is logical and mostly effective, showing good integration across strands. Minor gaps (no sensitivity testing, limited counter-arguments) keep it short of the top band, so 4 marks are appropriate.

The research question is addressed explicitly in the conclusion section, leaving no ambiguity. The answer is direct and supported, satisfying the âexplicitly answers research questionâ criterion for 3 marks.

Three well-chosen frameworksâAnsoff, Porterâs Generic Strategies and Hofstedeâare each appropriate to strategic, competitive and cultural facets of the seating decision. While limited in number, they cover distinct perspectives effectively and are not superficial add-ons, matching the âeffective selectionâ descriptor (4 marks).

The conclusion clearly states that Southwest should adopt assigned seating and summarises evidence from each analytical tool, mirroring points made earlier without contradiction. This full consistency with preceding analysis meets the highest descriptor (3 marks).

The submission maintains a clear, organisation-specific focus. Financial data, market-share figures, investor pressure from Elliott, and quotations from Southwest executives demonstrate sustained linkage. Nonetheless, occasional generic statements about consumer behaviour in aviation are not always re-anchored to Southwest. The depth and frequency of direct references justify âstrong analysis with partial integrationâ, aligning with band 4.

The student repeatedly references the concept of change, explaining why a shift from open seating to assigned seating is necessary and linking the idea to investor pressure and evolving consumer preferences. The concept is defined, contextualised and revisited in the analytical sections, yet it is not woven seamlessly through every argument; some tool-based discussion (e.g., Ansoff, Porter) proceeds without explicitly tying points back to the broader notion of change. This reflects a strong, but only partially integrated, use of the key concept â consistent with the 4-mark descriptor.

annotations.comment: The Key Concept âChangeâ is defined clearly and revisited in analysis, though consider explicitly weaving this concept into each framework discussion to deepen integration.
Stating loss of unit cost advantage is important; consider including comparative cost data against Delta or American to quantify the disadvantage.
Labeling the strategy as âmoderately low riskâ needs a risk assessment or sensitivity analysis to support this qualitative ranking.
The research question is prominently stated and clearly aligns with the investigationâs scope, facilitating a focused analysis throughout the assessment.
Personal code is provided clearly on the cover page, meeting IB presentation requirements for identification.
The table of contents is comprehensive and logically structured, enhancing navigation and reflecting a clear organizational flow.
Supporting documents are clearly listed and labelled, demonstrating appropriate quantity (five) and aligning with the appendix references used in analysis.
Verify that the listed page numbers in the ToC align precisely with the actual content pages to avoid navigation inconsistencies.
Use of current marketâshare figures provides strong data selection, situating Southwestâs position in context and supporting the background analysis effectively.
The sentence ânotwithstanding posting a record revenueâ is awkward. Rephrase to clarify that revenue rose yet a net loss was incurred, improving readability.
When introducing Elliottâs proposals, explicitly connect these initiatives to the concept of change to maintain thematic integration.
The definition of change is accurate, but sourcing it from Course Hero may weaken academic rigor. Consider an IB or scholarly source instead.
annotations.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

annotations.comment: The Sourcing & Supporting Documents description cuts off mid-sentence. Complete the explanation of document selection criteria for clarity.
In the Methodology header, specify the analytical approach (e.g., criteria for tool selection, data validation steps) to strengthen transparency.
The rationale for selecting Ansoff, Porterâs, and Hofstede is wellâstated, showing effective coverage of strategic, competitive, and cultural perspectives.
annotations.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

annotations.comment: Consider discussing why alternative frameworks (e.g., PESTEL or VRIO) were excluded to reinforce the rigour of tool selection.
annotations.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

annotations.comment: The âTools usedâ header is clear and signposts analytical content well, contributing to coherent structure throughout the IA.
The Ansoff Matrix is described accurately; however, cite original work (e.g., Ansoffâs 1957 article) to strengthen theoretical grounding.
annotations.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

annotations.comment: Figure 1 is legible and captioned, but including axes labels or a brief legend on risk scales would improve reader interpretation.
The discussion of market penetration strategy demonstrates clear application of Ansoff and logical link to Southwestâs context.
The reliance on Hoang for multiple footnotes indicates limited source variety. Diversify your supporting documents to enhance academic depth.
When invoking Hofstede, justify why only three dimensions are examined and explain how omitted dimensions might affect your conclusions.
annotations.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

annotations.comment: Integration of supporting documents to substantiate cost and revenue references is effective and strengthens data selection.
Reproducing Porterâs matrix is correct, but highlighting Southwestâs current positioning within the matrix would personalize the analysis.
annotations.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

annotations.comment: Identification of cost leadership as Southwestâs historic strategy is wellâsubstantiated and aligns effectively with Porterâs framework.
